feed.title: Welcome to the Center for Responsible AI at New York University
feed.subtitle: Center for Responsible AI at New York University.

entry.title: R/AI Hosting the New York RAI Research Day

link.title: R/AI Hosting the New York RAI Research Day

entry.summary: Visit the RAI Research Day page to learn more.

entry.title: AI-readiness for Official Data and Statistics | UN Headquarters

link.title: AI-readiness for Official Data and Statistics | UN Headquarters

entry.content:
On February 6, Julia Stoyanovich, delivered a keynote at United Nations Headquarters as part of the Friday Seminar on Emerging Issues titled AI-Readiness for Official Data and Statistics. The event brought together the community of official statistics, including national chief statisticians from UN Member States, directors and international cooperation staff, as well as chief statisticians of international agencies.

Her address, “Understanding the Technology: A Review of the Rapidly Evolving Uses of AI, from Data Production to Data Insights,” examined how modern AI systems interact with numerical data and metadata. As AI increasingly becomes an interface for accessing and analyzing information, the talk explored the implications for how official statistics are produced, interpreted, and disseminated.

Prof. Stoyanovich highlighted the importance of ensuring that official data remain accurate, properly attributed, and clearly contextualized as AI systems become more integrated into information ecosystems. Maintaining these qualities, she emphasized, is essential to preserving the integrity and public value of official statistics.

🎥 Watch the recording on the UN WebTV channel: https://webtv.un.org/en/asset/k1z/k1z77fpw1i
entry.summary: AI-readiness for Official Data and Statistics

entry.title: AI Governance for Youth and Society | United Nations Side Event

link.title: AI Governance for Youth and Society | United Nations Side Event

entry.content:
On February 6, Julia Stoyanovich, spoke at a United Nations side event during the 64th session of the Commission for Social Development (CSocD64) titled “Shaping the AI Future: Ethical and Inclusive AI Governance for Youth and All.”

In her presentation, “The Joys of Doing, Learning and Being,” Prof. Stoyanovich reflected on what it means to grow up and pursue knowledge in an age of increasingly capable AI systems. Drawing on the animated short Happy Birthd-AI from the Center’s Algorithmia series, she discussed how powerful AI tools can support learning while also raising questions about overreliance on automation.

The talk emphasized the importance of preserving curiosity, creativity, and human judgment as AI becomes more embedded in everyday life, particularly for young people navigating education and technology in a rapidly evolving digital environment.

🎥 Watch the recording: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=AOoVQLXNmkk
entry.summary: AI Governance for Youth and Society

entry.title: Building a Fairer Future for NYC

link.title: Building a Fairer Future for NYC

entry.content:
On February 3, NYU and CUNY convened Building a Fairer Future for NYC, a conversation bringing together leaders from academia, industry, and government to explore how technology and innovation can support a safer and more equitable city. R/AI faculty participated actively in the program. Julia Stoyanovich, Director of the NYU Center for Responsible AI, delivered a keynote and joined the New York AI Exchange panel, while Emily Black a faculty member of the Center, took part in the discussion “Does Progress in AI Lead to NYC’s Economic Development?”

In her keynote, “Responsible AI Beyond the Principles: Building a Regime of Distributed Accountability,” Prof. Stoyanovich discussed how responsibility in AI systems is shared across designers, deployers, policymakers, and institutions. Drawing on real-world examples from city technologies, she emphasized that effective governance requires transparency, education, and clear policy frameworks to ensure that AI systems support the public interest.
entry.summary: Building a Fairer Future for NYC

entry.title: R/AI Research for Ukrainian Scholars – Fall 2025 Showcase

link.title: R/AI Research for Ukrainian Scholars – Fall 2025 Showcase

entry.content:
Another Semester of the Responsible AI for Ukraine Program Wraps Up with a Project Showcase!

RAIforUkraine is a program run by the Center for Responsible AI at the Tandon School of Engineering at New York University, in close collaboration with Ukrainian Catholic University in Lviv, Ukraine. Its goals are to help build research capacity in Ukraine, both generally in STEM (Science, Technology, Engineering, and Mathematics) and specifically in the critically important area of Responsible AI, and to catalyze the integration of Ukrainian researchers into the global research community.

On December 12, R/AI hosted the Fall 2025 Responsible AI for Ukraine Project Showcase, bringing together research fellows, mentors, partners, and members of the global Responsible AI community.

During the event, 20 research teams presented the projects they developed throughout the semester. The work spanned the program’s core themes—including algorithmic fairness, interpretability, privacy, data management for machine learning, and evaluation and benchmarking—while also exploring emerging directions such as multilingual misinformation detection in the context of the Russian–Ukrainian war, AI-assisted species identification in underwater imagery, and causal machine learning applications in oncology.

The showcase highlighted the progress made by the Fall 2025 cohort and offered a preview of future research directions and publications emerging from the program. Eleven teams demonstrating strong progress will continue their work in Spring 2026 with support from program stipends.

If you missed the live event, you can now watch the recording on our YouTube channel.
